description: Tower Perspective governance registry. Maps Copilot admin controls to compliance frameworks (SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP, EU AI Act). Compliance control IDs verified 2026-04-12 against official framework documents. Admin control metadata verified against docs.github.com.

soc2.name: SOC 2 Type II
soc2.note: Controls use the AICPA Trust Services Criteria (CC = Common Criteria, A = Availability, C = Confidentiality, PI = Processing Integrity, P = Privacy).

iso27001.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
iso27001.note: Annex A controls (A.5 - A.18). The 2022 revision uses a flat numbering scheme.

hipaa.name: HIPAA Security Rule
hipaa.note: 45 CFR 164.308 (admin), 164.310 (physical), 164.312 (technical). Requires signed BAA for covered entities.

fedramp.name: FedRAMP
fedramp.note: Status per Copilot offering (In Process / Moderate / High). Verify current authorization state before claiming.

euAiAct.name: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
euAiAct.note: Risk category determines obligations. General-purpose AI models have separate Chapter V rules.

controls.governanceNote: Org owners toggle individual Copilot features (chat, suggestions, MCP, agent mode) on or off at the organization level. Foundational control — everything else depends on this being set correctly first.
Repository-level and path-level exclusions prevent Copilot from indexing sensitive code. Essential for orgs with regulated workloads or third-party proprietary code in monorepos.

GitHub's indemnification for suggestions matching public code. Depends on the suggestions-matching-public-code filter being set to Block mode. Not a standalone toggle — it is a legal benefit that follows from enabling the filter. Legal should review the indemnity terms before relying on it.
Immutable log of Copilot events (suggestion acceptance, chat sessions, policy changes, seat assignments). The foundation of every SOC 2 evidence pack.
Adoption, acceptance rate, active-seat trends. Required for license optimization and ROI reporting.
Assign, unassign and audit Copilot seats. Tie unassignment to the HR offboarding workflow — orphaned seats are a direct security finding.

Data residency requires adopting GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency (GHE.com) — a separate product SKU, not a settings toggle. Pins repo data to an EU data plane but does NOT cover Copilot prompt/completion traffic. Required for Schrems II compliance in many EU orgs. See tower.html#sovereign=ghe-data-residency for the full deployment options matrix.
Plan tier determines which governance controls are available at all. Business and Enterprise unlock audit logs, content exclusion and policy management.
Copilot-powered security scanning on code suggestions. Must be explicitly enabled at the org level. Pair with the downstream Copilot Autofix workflow for closed-loop remediation.

Auto-generates PRs that fix Code Scanning (CodeQL) alerts. Enabled by default for repos using CodeQL. Governed at enterprise/org/repo level under Advanced Security settings. Guard with branch-protection requiring human approval — fix quality is model-dependent.

Copilot code review — AI-powered PR review triggered manually or automatically via branch rulesets. Must be explicitly enabled as a feature policy at org/enterprise level. Automatic triggering requires repo-level ruleset configuration. Useful signal but NEVER a substitute for independent human review on security-sensitive paths.

Route Copilot traffic through your own Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google Cloud tenant. Enterprise admins add keys at enterprise level; org admins at org level. Public preview since Nov 2025. Gives control over retention, billing and regional pinning — at the cost of complexity. See tower.html#sovereign=byok-enterprise for EU deployment strategies per provider.

Model Context Protocol grants the agent access to external tools. MCP Allowlist Governance is now GA (April 2026) — enterprise admins can explicitly allowlist which MCP servers are permitted, controlling data exposure per org. Highest-blast-radius control in the catalog.

Two mechanisms: (1) per-repo .github/copilot-instructions.md file (developer convention, no admin toggle), and (2) org-level custom instructions field in Copilot settings (admin-controlled). Both define coding standards, security guardrails and forbidden patterns. Treat as security configuration — review in PRs, lock with CODEOWNERS.

Controls whether Copilot blocks or allows suggestions that match public GitHub code. Setting to Block is required for IP indemnity protection. Essential privacy control for orgs shipping proprietary software.

Controls access to partner cloud agents (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI Codex, etc.). Each provider can be toggled independently. Off by default — must be explicitly enabled. Extends the blast radius beyond GitHub-hosted models.
Controls access to premium and extended models beyond the default. May incur additional cost per premium request. Governs which frontier models your seats can reach.
Opt-in toggle for preview-stage Copilot features on Business/Enterprise plans. Off by default — orgs must explicitly opt in. Review the preview terms before enabling in regulated environments.
Enables pay-per-use premium requests for org members without a Copilot license (e.g. for Copilot code review on their PRs). Off by default. Requires budget awareness — costs scale with usage.

Autopilot runs Agent Mode without step-by-step confirmations. Preview since April 2026, VS Code only. Organizations should evaluate whether fully autonomous execution aligns with their change management policies before enabling.

SOC2.statusNote: GitHub Copilot Business and Enterprise are covered by GitHub's SOC 2 Type II report. Request the report via the GitHub Trust Center.
evidencePackChecklist: Seat assignment history with offboarding evidence
Incident response runbook with drill records

ISO27001.statusNote: GitHub maintains ISO 27001 certification covering Copilot Business and Enterprise.

GDPR.statusNote: GitHub publishes a Data Protection Addendum and Standard Contractual Clauses. EU Data Residency adds a regional-pinning option for Business/Enterprise.

keyArticles.Art25: Data protection by design and by default
keyArticles.Art28: Processor obligations (use BYOK for full processor control)

dpiaChecklist: Document lawful basis for training data processing
Map international data flows and SCC coverage

HIPAA.statusNote: GitHub can sign a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) for Copilot Enterprise only. PHI must never be included in prompts or code comments even with a BAA in place — treat Copilot as a high-sensitivity endpoint.

rules.164.312: Technical Safeguards (access control, audit, integrity, transmission)

FedRAMP.statusNote: GitHub Copilot authorization status is evolving. Verify current status in the FedRAMP Marketplace before claiming compliance in a federal procurement.
FedRAMP.note: This entry intentionally ships unverified — treat as informational until a GitHub representative confirms current authorization.

EU_AI_ACT.statusNote: Copilot is a general-purpose AI system under Chapter V. Deployers (orgs using Copilot) have transparency and human oversight obligations that vary by use case.
deployerObligations: Inform users when interacting with AI-generated content
Human oversight for high-risk code paths
Log model outputs on regulated workflows
Incident reporting to national supervisory authority for serious incidents

incidents.title: Token leaked via suggestion
incidents.trigger: Secret scanning alert fires on a public push that contains a Copilot-suggested key, or a grep on audit logs surfaces a completion containing a credential.
detection: GitHub Secret Scanning alert on push
Audit log keyword match on credential patterns
Third-party IAM anomaly (unknown source IP using a valid key)
containment: Revoke the leaked credential at the provider
Invalidate any repo clones containing the leaked commit (force-push history rewrite, notify forks)
Suspend the affected Copilot seat pending review
forensics: Export audit log window (±4 hours around the event)
Correlate with Copilot suggestion acceptance events
Check whether Security Scanning fired earlier and was dismissed
Identify whether content exclusion would have prevented this
postIncident: Add the credential pattern to org secret-scanning custom patterns
Enable content exclusion on paths containing credentials
Review whether .github/copilot-instructions.md warns against inlining secrets
Schedule a blameless review within 7 days

incidents.title: Prompt injection exfiltration via agent mode
incidents.trigger: Agent mode completes a task via an MCP tool call that references data the user did not intentionally share (e.g. the agent read an issue body containing an injection payload and then called a webhook tool).
detection: Outbound request to unfamiliar domain from an MCP-enabled session
Unexpected tool invocation sequence in audit logs
PR description containing data from a file the agent should not have had access to
containment: Disable MCP at the org level until the offending server is identified
Revoke the specific MCP server's credentials
Quarantine any agent-generated PRs pending review
Notify affected users whose data may have been touched
forensics: Dump the agent's full tool call history for the session
Identify the injection source (issue comment, file, tool output)
Test whether the same payload reproduces on a clean environment
postIncident: Add the untrusted-source pattern to the MCP sandbox policy
Review MCP server allowlist and remove high-blast-radius servers
Enable agent self-review gating on this repository
Share anonymized IOCs with the community

incidents.title: MCP server supply chain compromise
incidents.trigger: A popular MCP server publishes a malicious update that exfiltrates the agent's environment variables or tool call arguments to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
detection: Community CVE or security advisory for an installed MCP server
Anomalous egress traffic from a machine running the MCP server
Checksum mismatch on an MCP server binary at startup
containment: Disable all MCP servers organization-wide via feature policies
Uninstall the compromised package from every affected machine
Rotate all credentials that were present in the MCP server's environment
Block the attacker's exfiltration endpoints at the network egress
forensics: Collect the compromised package's source and diff against the last known-good version
Review audit logs for any tool calls that referenced secrets
Check CI/CD systems for propagation (the MCP server may be bundled in pipelines)
Identify the time window of exposure
postIncident: Pin all MCP server versions in configuration (no floating tags)
Require signed releases for any MCP server in the org allowlist
Subscribe to security advisories for every approved MCP server
Add MCP supply chain to the threat model in the Security X-Ray
